ENGAA-2020-M1-001: The admission charge to a cinema is different for adults and children. Admission for 2 adults and 3 children costs £20. Admission for 4 adults and 4 children costs £34. What does admission cost for 6 adults and 2 children?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-001: A soldering iron has a copper tip of mass 2.0g. The tip is heated with 30W of thermal power. In 50s, the temperature of the tip increases by 200°C. How much energy is transferred from the tip to the surroundings in this time? (specific heat capacity of copper = 400 J kg⁻¹ °C⁻¹)
ENGAA-2020-M1-002: A fair spinner has eight equal sections: three sections labelled 1, one section labelled 2, and four sections labelled 3. The spinner is spun twice, and the two numbers scored are added. What is the probability that the sum of the two numbers is 5?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-002: Uranium-238 (²³⁸₉₂U) decays by a series of alpha and beta⁻ emissions to become the stable isotope lead-206 (²⁰⁶₈₂Pb). How many beta⁻ particles are emitted in the decay of one uranium-238 nucleus to lead-206?
ENGAA-2020-M1-003: Consider the four lines with the following equations: (1) $2x+6y=3$, (2) $9y=3x-4$, (3) $2y=6x+3$, (4) $4x+6y-9=0$. Which two lines are perpendicular?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-003: A dc electricity transmission system uses an undersea cable to send electricity from one country to another. The first country supplies electricity at a voltage of 400kV and 2000A. The second country receives electricity at 160kV and 4000A. What is the percentage efficiency of the system and how much energy is wasted every minute?
ENGAA-2020-M2-001: Find the sum of the solutions of $2\left(\frac{x}{4}+3\right)^2 - \left(\frac{x}{4}+3\right) - 36 = 0$.
ENGAA-2020-PHY-004: Two fixed horizontal metal rails are side by side and 12cm apart, connected to a dc power supply by a switch that is initially open. A freely moveable metal rod of length 20cm lies across the rails at 90° to them (only the 12cm between the rails carries current). [DIAGRAM: rod on rails viewed from above, uniform magnetic field 0.50T perpendicular into the page]. The rod has weight 0.40N. The switch is closed, giving a current of 2.4A in the circuit, and the rod moves. What is the initial magnitude of the acceleration of the rod and what is its direction? (g = 10 N kg⁻¹)
ENGAA-2020-M2-002: When the expression $(2x+3)^2 - (x-3)^2$ is written in the form $p(x+q)^2 + r$, where $p$, $q$ and $r$ are constants, what is the value of $r$?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-005: Two trolleys move towards each other along a straight horizontal track. One trolley has mass 8.0kg travelling right at 4.0 m/s. The other has mass 2.0kg travelling left at 1.0 m/s. When they collide they stick together. How much kinetic energy is transferred to other forms of energy in the collision?
ENGAA-2020-M1-004: The number of pairs of winter boots sold on a day is inversely proportional to the cube of the outside temperature that day (in °C). On a day when the temperature is 8°C, 250 pairs are sold. The next day, when the temperature is x°C, the number sold is 700% more than the previous day. What is the value of x?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-006: A car of mass 800kg travels in a straight line along a horizontal road. The car accelerates non-uniformly from rest for 5.0 seconds and then moves at constant speed. [DIAGRAM: distance-time graph showing distance = 10m at t = 5s, and distance = 40m at t = 10s]. What is the average resultant force acting on the car over the time for which it is accelerating?
ENGAA-2020-M1-005: In a sale, all prices are reduced by 25%. A customer calculates the pre-sale price of a bicycle incorrectly by increasing the marked sale price by 25%. The customer's calculated pre-sale price is incorrect by £15. What is the correct pre-sale price of the bicycle?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-007: P and Q are two fixed points on the surface of the ocean, 6.0m apart. An ocean wave travels from P to Q with frequency 0.50Hz at constant speed. A wave peak passes Q at t=0s. The next wave peak travelling towards Q passes P at t=0.80s. What is the speed of the wave?
ENGAA-2020-M2-003: [DIAGRAM: QS is perpendicular to PR, with PSR a straight line; PS = x cm, PQ = y cm, QR = z cm, angle QRS = 61°]. Which one of the following is an expression for the length z, in cm?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-008: A parachutist of mass 80.0kg drops from a plane travelling at 40.0 m/s, 2000m above the Earth's surface. The parachutist hits the ground at 5.00 m/s. How much work is done by the parachutist against drag forces during the fall? (g = 10.0 N kg⁻¹)
ENGAA-2020-M2-004: Two vertices of a square are at (1,1) and (3,5). What is the difference between the perimeters of the largest and smallest possible squares that can be drawn with these points as two of their vertices?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-009:
A light spring of unstretched length 0.10 m has a spring constant of 20 N m$^{-1}$. The spring is suspended so that it is vertical and a load of mass 0.050 kg is attached to the end of the spring.
The load is pulled vertically downwards until the length of the spring is 0.30 m. The load is then released.
What is the speed of the load at the instant that the spring returns to its unstretched length?
(gravitational field strength = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; assume that resistive forces are negligible)
ENGAA-2020-M2-005: The quadratic equation $2x^2-px-4=0$, where $p$ is a positive constant, has two solutions that differ by 6. What is the value of $p$?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-010: A rocket travelling in space is burning its fuel at a constant rate. By expelling the burnt fuel through a nozzle, the engine applies a constant force to the rocket. What is happening to the magnitude of the acceleration of the rocket?
ENGAA-2020-M2-006: $(x-1)$ and $(x-2)$ are both factors of $x^4+ax^3+bx^2-12x+4$. What are the values of $a$ and $b$?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-011: A block of mass $m$ slides down a rough slope. At position 1 the velocity is $v_1$; at position 2, a vertical distance $h$ below position 1, the velocity is $v_2$. Which expression gives the work done against friction by the block as it slides from position 1 to position 2? (g = gravitational field strength; air resistance negligible)
ENGAA-2020-M2-007: What is the area of the region enclosed between the curve $y=\frac{1}{2}x^2$, the line $y=-x$, and the lines $x=1$ and $x=3$?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-012: A car braking system has two cylinders (piston X, diameter 4.0cm; piston Y, diameter 12.0cm) linked by a flexible tube containing incompressible oil. The driver exerts a force of 36.0N on piston X, which moves 5.4cm. What is the resultant force on piston Y and how far does it move?
ENGAA-2020-M2-008: A line with non-zero gradient $m$ is reflected in the line $y=x$. What is the gradient of the reflected line?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-013: [DIAGRAM: displacement-time graph of a wave in air, time axis in units of 10⁻³s, showing the wave's period]. The speed of the wave in air is 300 m/s. The wave travels into water, where (wave speed in water)/(wave speed in air) = 0.2. What is the wavelength of the wave in water?
ENGAA-2020-M2-009: The sum of the first 20 terms of an arithmetic progression is 50. The sum of the next 20 terms of the arithmetic progression is -50. What is the sum of the first 100 terms of the arithmetic progression?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-014: A flat rectangular coil of wire (sides 30cm and 20cm, 50 turns) is freely pivoted about an axle through the middle of the 30cm sides. Part of the coil sits between the poles of a U-shaped magnet (poles 4.0cm long, field uniform between poles and zero elsewhere). [DIAGRAM: coil between magnet poles, axle along the 30cm side midline]. The current is 0.60A and the magnetic flux density is 0.050T. What is the moment about the axle produced by the magnetic force acting on the coil?
ENGAA-2020-M2-010: The line $L$ with equation $y=mx+c$, where $m>0$ and $c\ge0$, passes through (2,4). A line is drawn through (2,4) perpendicular to $L$. The triangle enclosed between the two lines and the y-axis has area 5 square units. What is the larger of the two possible values of $m$?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-015: A box of mass 3.0kg is pulled 5.0m directly up a smooth slope by a constant applied force of 30N acting parallel to the slope. The initial speed is 3.0 m/s and the final speed is 7.0 m/s, with constant acceleration. What is the component of the weight acting down the slope? (g = 10 N/kg; air resistance negligible)
ENGAA-2020-M2-011: P and Q are two different geometric progressions. The 3rd term of each is 4. The 5th term of each is 2. What is the modulus of the difference between the sums to infinity of P and Q?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-016: Electrical power is supplied through a dc transmission line of two metal wires, each 8.0km long with cross-sectional area 1.0cm². The resistivity of the metal is 2.5×10⁻⁷ Ωm. Power is transmitted at a potential difference of 24000V, with 120kW supplied to the line. At what rate is energy being wasted as heat in the wires?
ENGAA-2020-M2-012: The curve $y = x^3 + 3\sqrt5\,px^2 + 3px + 13$ has two distinct turning points. What are all the possible values of $p$?
ENGAA-2020-PHY-017: A tennis ball travelling at 24.0 m/s is hit by a racket. It returns back along its original path, having undergone a change in velocity of 48.0 m/s. The acceleration while in contact with the racket is constant with magnitude 6000 m/s². What is the total distance travelled by the ball whilst in contact with the racket?
ENGAA-2020-M2-013: Find the number of solutions of the equation $14\cos^3x + 10\sin^2x\cos x = 13\cos x$ in the range $-2\pi \le x \le 2\pi$.
ENGAA-2020-PHY-018: [DIAGRAM: two identical light springs in series supporting loads of 0.20kg and 1.0kg in one arrangement; the same two springs connected in parallel supporting a 2.0kg mass in another arrangement, with a shown extension of 4.0cm in the first case]. In the parallel arrangement, what is the total strain energy in the springs? (g = 10 N/kg; springs obey Hooke's law)
ENGAA-2020-M2-014: Find the product of the real roots of the equation $(\log_{10}x^2)^2 + \log_{10}x = 3$.
ENGAA-2020-PHY-019: An object of mass 2.5kg is at rest at t=0s. A resultant force acts on the object in a constant direction. [DIAGRAM: force-time graph, force axis marked 0, 8, 16, 24 N; time axis marked 0.04, 0.08, 0.12, 0.16, 0.20 s]. What is the kinetic energy of the object at t=0.20s?
ENGAA-2020-M2-015: Find the maximum value of the gradient of the curve with equation $y = 2 - 4x + 4x^{3/2} - x^2$, where $x>0$.
ENGAA-2020-PHY-020: [DIAGRAM: circuit with two cells (6.0V and 16V), an ammeter, and resistors of 100Ω, 1200Ω, 200Ω and R arranged in a bridge-like network]. The ammeter records a current of zero. What is the resistance of resistor R?
